config.name: USB_USE_CONFIGURATIONS
config.brief: Support for user-provided USB hardware configurations.
config.note: When enabled the user must provide a variable named @p usb_configurations of type @p usb_configurations_t.

macro.name: _usb_isr_invoke_event_cb
macro.brief: Common ISR code, USB event notification.
macro.param: Pointer to the USB driver instance.

macro.name: _usb_isr_invoke_sof_cb
macro.param: Pointer to the USB driver instance.

macro.name: _usb_isr_invoke_setup_cb
macro.brief: Common ISR code, setup packet callback.
macro.param: Pointer to the USB driver instance.

macro.name: _usb_isr_invoke_in_cb
macro.brief: Common ISR code, IN endpoint callback.
macro.param: Pointer to the USB driver instance.

macro.name: _usb_isr_invoke_out_cb
macro.brief: Common ISR code, OUT endpoint callback.
macro.param: Pointer to the USB driver instance.

typedef.name: hal_usb_driver_c
typedef.brief: Type of structure representing a USB driver.

typedef.name: hal_usb_config_t
typedef.brief: Type of structure representing a USB hardware configuration.

struct.name: hal_usb_config
struct.note: Protocol and class behavior are configured separately through the USB binder object.

fields.verbatim: /* End of the mandatory fields.*/ usb_lld_config_fields;

typedef.name: hal_usb_service_info_t
typedef.brief: Type of structure representing USB service ownership metadata.

struct.name: hal_usb_service_info
struct.details: This structure identifies the interfaces and endpoints managed by a USB service instance. Endpoint zero is never owned by a service.

field.name: if_base
field.brief: First interface number owned by the service.

field.name: if_count
field.brief: Number of consecutive interfaces owned by the service.

field.name: in_ep_mask
field.brief: Bit mask of owned IN endpoints, bit N corresponds to endpoint N.

field.name: out_ep_mask
field.brief: Bit mask of owned OUT endpoints, bit N corresponds to endpoint N.

class.name: hal_usb_service
class.brief: Base class for composable USB services.
class.details: This class represents a reusable USB function or service managed by a USB binder. A service exposes static ownership metadata together with USB-specific lifecycle and dispatch hooks, but it does not own the USB peripheral lifecycle. The virtual methods carrying the @p I suffix must be invoked with the system lock already held by the caller.

field.name: binder
field.brief: Binder currently owning the service, can be @p NULL.

field.name: info
field.brief: Pointer to the immutable service ownership metadata.

field.name: next
field.brief: Next service in the binder registration list.

field.name: prev
field.brief: Previous service in the binder registration list.

method.name: usbServiceObjectInit
method.brief: Initializes the base USB service part of a derived object.
method.param: VMT of the concrete derived service.

method.name: usbServiceGetBinderX
method.brief: Returns the current binder owning the service.

method.name: usbServiceOwnsInterfaceX
method.brief: Checks whether the service owns a specific interface number.

method.name: usbServiceOwnsInEndpointX
method.brief: Checks whether the service owns a specific IN endpoint.

method.name: usbServiceOwnsOutEndpointX
method.brief: Checks whether the service owns a specific OUT endpoint.

class.name: hal_usb_binder
class.brief: Base class for USB device binders.
class.details: A binder is the device-composition object sitting between a USB driver instance and one or more USB services. The binder owns the final descriptors and dispatches USB events to the registered services. The virtual methods carrying the @p I suffix must be invoked with the system lock already held by the caller.

field.name: usbp
field.brief: Currently bound USB driver, can be @p NULL.

field.name: services
field.brief: Head of the registered services list.

field.name: service_last
field.brief: Tail of the registered services list.

method.name: usbBinderOnResetI
usbBinderOnConfigureI
usbBinderOnUnconfigureI
usbBinderOnSuspendI
usbBinderOnWakeupI
usbBinderOnSOFI
usbBinderOnInI
usbBinderOnOutI
usbBinderOnSetup
usbBinderObjectInit
method.brief: Initializes the base USB binder part of a derived object.
method.param: VMT of the concrete derived binder.

method.name: usbBinderGetDriverX
method.brief: Returns the currently bound USB driver.
method.return: The bound USB driver or @p NULL.

method.name: usbBinderGetFirstServiceX
method.return: Pointer to the first service or @p NULL.

method.name: usbBinderGetServicesNumX
method.brief: Returns the number of registered services.

method.name: usbBind
method.brief: Attaches a USB binder to the USB driver.

method.name: usbUnbind
method.brief: Detaches the currently bound USB binder.

method.name: usbConnectBus
method.brief: Connects the USB device to the bus.

method.name: usbDisconnectBus
method.brief: Disconnects the USB device from the bus.

method.name: usbInitEndpointI
usbDisableEndpointsI
method.brief: Disables all active endpoints except endpoint zero.

method.name: usbReadSetupI
method.brief: Reads a setup packet from endpoint zero.

method.name: usbStartReceiveI
method.brief: Starts a receive transaction on an OUT endpoint.

method.name: usbStartTransmitI
method.brief: Starts a transmit transaction on an IN endpoint.

method.name: usbReceive
usbTransmit
usbEp0WaitSetup
method.brief: Waits for a new endpoint-zero setup packet.
method.return: @p MSG_OK if a setup packet is available.

method.name: usbEp0Reply
method.brief: Replies to the current endpoint-zero request.
method.implementation:
osalDbgCheck(self != NULL);
return ep0_reply_or_ack(self, buf, n);

method.name: usbEp0Receive
method.brief: Receives the data phase of the current endpoint-zero request.
method.implementation:
osalDbgCheck(self != NULL);
return ep0_receive_or_status(self, buf, n);

method.name: usbEp0Stall
usbEp0HandleStandardRequest
method.brief: Handles the current standard endpoint-zero request.

method.name: usbStallReceiveI
method.return: @p true if the endpoint is busy and was not stalled.

method.name: usbStallTransmitI
method.return: @p true if the endpoint is busy and was not stalled.

method.name: usbGetDriverStateX
method.brief: Returns the current USB driver state.

method.name: usbGetBinderX
method.return: The current USB binder or @p NULL.

method.name: usbGetEventsX
method.brief: Returns the cached USB event flags.

method.name: usbGetAndClearEventsX
method.brief: Gets and clears cached USB event flags.
method.param: Mask of events to be returned and cleared.

method.name: usbGetFrameNumberX
method.brief: Returns the current USB frame number.

method.name: usbGetReceiveTransactionSizeX
method.brief: Returns the received size of the last OUT transaction.

function.name: usbServiceBind
function.brief: Binds a USB service to a USB binder.

function.name: usbServiceUnbind
function.brief: Unbinds a USB service from its binder.

function.name: usbServiceResetI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of a reset event.

function.name: usbServiceConfigureI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of a configuration event.

function.name: usbServiceUnconfigureI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of an unconfigure event.

function.name: usbServiceSuspendI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of a suspend event.

function.name: usbServiceWakeupI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of a wake-up event.

function.name: usbServiceSOFI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of a start-of-frame event.

function.name: usbServiceInI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of an IN endpoint event.

function.name: usbServiceOutI
function.brief: Notifies a USB service of an OUT endpoint event.

function.name: usbServiceSetup
function.brief: Dispatches an endpoint-zero request to a USB service.

function.name: usbBinderBind
function.brief: Binds a USB binder to a USB driver.

function.name: usbBinderUnbind
function.brief: Unbinds a USB binder from its USB driver.

function.name: usbBinderRegisterService
function.brief: Registers a USB service on a binder.

function.name: usbBinderUnregisterService
function.brief: Unregisters a USB service from a binder.

function.name: usbBinderGetNextServiceX
function.brief: Returns the next registered service.
function.return: Pointer to the next service or @p NULL.

function.name: usbBinderGetDescriptor
function.brief: Requests a descriptor from a USB binder.

function.name: usbBinderResetI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of a reset event.

function.name: usbBinderConfigureI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of a configuration event.

function.name: usbBinderUnconfigureI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of an unconfigure event.

function.name: usbBinderSuspendI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of a suspend event.

function.name: usbBinderWakeupI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of a wake-up event.

function.name: usbBinderSOFI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of a start-of-frame event.

function.name: usbBinderInI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of an IN endpoint event.

function.name: usbBinderOutI
function.brief: Notifies a USB binder of an OUT endpoint event.

function.name: usbBinderSetup
function.brief: Dispatches an endpoint-zero request through a USB binder.

function.name: usbInit
function.brief: USB Driver initialization.
function.implementation: usb_lld_init();

function.name: _usb_reset
function.brief: Common USB reset handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: _usb_suspend
function.brief: Common USB suspend handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: _usb_wakeup
function.brief: Common USB wake-up handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: _usb_ep0setup
function.brief: Common endpoint-zero SETUP handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: _usb_ep0in
function.brief: Common endpoint-zero IN handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: _usb_ep0out
function.brief: Common endpoint-zero OUT handler invoked by the LLD.

function.name: get_hword
usbbnd_if_conflict
usbbnd_service_conflict
usb_invoke_event_cb
function.implementation: usbp->events |= flags;

function.name: setup_reset
setup_error
ep0_resume_waiterI
function.implementation: osalThreadResumeI(&usbp->ep0thread, msg);

function.name: ep0_signal_resetI
ep0_signal_setupI
set_address_thread
ep0_reply_or_ack
ep0_receive_or_status
